questions.q: What kind of natural feature is the Sarayu in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Sarayu is the sacred river that flows past the city of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Which city stands on the banks of the Sarayu river?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya, the capital of Kosala and seat of the Ikshvaku dynasty, lies on the Sarayu.

questions.q: How is the Sarayu generally regarded in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Sarayu is revered as a sacred and holy river throughout the epic.

questions.q: Whose birth city was located on the Sarayu?
questions.explanation: Rama was born in Ayodhya, the city that sits beside the Sarayu river.

questions.q: Which dynasty's capital, Ayodhya, lay on the Sarayu?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya on the Sarayu was the seat of the Ikshvaku, or Solar, dynasty into which Rama was born.

questions.q: When Rama left for his exile, which river did he and his companions need to cross?
questions.explanation: Rama's departure into exile took him across the Sarayu away from Ayodhya.

questions.q: In which kingdom does the Sarayu flow in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: The Sarayu flows through Kosala, the kingdom whose capital was Ayodhya.

questions.q: The Sarayu is described as a witness to which major event in Rama's life?
questions.explanation: The river is portrayed as a witness to Rama's birth at Ayodhya on its banks.

questions.q: At the end of his life, where did Rama go to depart from the world?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Rama walked into the Sarayu river to leave the mortal world.

questions.q: What is the Sarayu most closely associated with for the people of Ayodhya?
options: Their daily life and sacred rites
questions.explanation: The Sarayu was central to the daily life and sacred rituals of Ayodhya's people.

questions.q: When Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana left Ayodhya in exile, who drove the chariot that took them to and across the Sarayu region?
questions.explanation: Sumantra, Dasharatha's charioteer and minister, drove Rama's party out of Ayodhya toward the Sarayu.

questions.q: After crossing beyond the Sarayu region, at which river did Guha the Nishada chief ferry Rama across?
questions.explanation: Guha, the Nishada king, arranged a boat to ferry Rama's party across the Ganga during the exile journey.

questions.q: On the very first night after leaving Ayodhya, on the bank of which river did Rama's party halt?
questions.explanation: Rama's group spent its first night of exile on the bank of the Tamasa, a stream near the Sarayu.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the Sarayu is said to arise from which celestial lake?
options: The Sarayu has no named source
questions.explanation: Tradition links the Sarayu's origin to the sacred Manasa (Manasarovar) lake.

questions.q: Which two sons did Rama and Sita have, who in tradition are associated with cities near the Sarayu/Kosala region?
questions.explanation: Lava and Kusha were the twin sons of Rama and Sita, born during her later years.

questions.q: Bathing in or drinking the waters of the Sarayu is traditionally believed to confer what?
questions.explanation: The Sarayu's sacred waters are believed to grant purification and spiritual merit.

questions.q: King Dasharatha, who ruled the city on the Sarayu, was the father of how many sons in all?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha of Ayodhya had four sons: Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: The Sarayu is described as flowing past Ayodhya and serving as a witness to Rama's return after how many years of exile?
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya on the Sarayu after completing fourteen years of exile.

questions.q: Which sage's hermitage and the boyhood education of Rama are connected to the Sarayu/Kosala land before his adventures began?
questions.explanation: Vasishtha was the royal preceptor of the Ikshvaku house at Ayodhya on the Sarayu and guided the young princes.

questions.q: When the sage Vishwamitra first took Rama and Lakshmana from Ayodhya, in which direction did they travel, crossing the Sarayu's confluence area?
options: Toward the forest and Mithila to perform tasks
Up into the Himalayas to meditate
questions.explanation: Vishwamitra led the young princes out from Ayodhya toward the forests and onward to Mithila.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, near where the Sarayu meets the Ganga, Vishwamitra recounted the story of which river goddess's descent?
questions.explanation: At the confluence of the Sarayu and Ganga, Vishwamitra narrated the famous story of Ganga's descent to earth.

questions.q: The Sarayu river city Ayodhya is praised in the Ramayana as being founded or associated with which ancient king?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits the founding of Ayodhya on the Sarayu to Manu, progenitor of the Ikshvaku line.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the name 'Sarayu' is connected to the bursting forth of waters from Lake Manasa caused by which figure?
options: Brahma, when the lake's waters broke out
questions.explanation: The tradition links the Sarayu's emergence to the waters of Lake Manasa, associated with Brahma, breaking forth as a river.

questions.q: Which celebrated ancestor of Rama brought the Ganga down to earth, whose waters tradition links with the Sarayu's region near Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha, an ancestor of Rama in the Ikshvaku line, brought the Ganga down to earth through his austerities.

questions.q: At the close of the Ramayana, when Rama entered the Sarayu, in what form is he said to have departed, accompanied by the people of Ayodhya?
options: He returned to his divine form as Vishnu
He turned into a stone idol
He sailed away on a ship
questions.explanation: Entering the Sarayu, Rama resumed his divine identity as Vishnu and ascended, with many of Ayodhya's people following him.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana's account of Rama's final departure into the Sarayu, which deity appeared to receive and accompany him?
questions.explanation: Brahma appeared at the Sarayu and welcomed Rama as he gave up his earthly body and returned to his divine abode.

questions.q: The Sarayu is sometimes identified in later tradition with which modern river of the Ayodhya region?
questions.explanation: The Sarayu of the epic is traditionally identified with the Ghaghara, also called the Saryu, near present-day Ayodhya.

questions.q: When Rama's party crossed the Sarayu's vicinity at the start of exile, the citizens of Ayodhya who had followed him were left behind sleeping near which river?
questions.explanation: Rama slipped away by night from the Tamasa's bank, leaving the loyal citizens of Ayodhya asleep so they would return home.

questions.q: Which charioteer-minister of Ayodhya returned alone with the empty chariot across the Sarayu region, grieving, after Rama crossed the Ganga?
questions.explanation: Sumantra was sent back with the chariot after Guha ferried Rama across the Ganga, returning sorrowfully to Ayodhya.

questions.q: In tradition, the place on the Sarayu where Rama is said to have entered the water to leave the world is venerated near Ayodhya as what kind of site?
options: A sacred ghat or tirtha (pilgrimage spot)
questions.explanation: The spot on the Sarayu linked to Rama's departure is revered as a sacred ghat and pilgrimage tirtha near Ayodhya.
